channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Aurora borealis returns: When and where to watch the northern lights
item.description: Sky gazers in several U.S. states could get a colorful glimpse of the northern lights as we enter the weekend, thanks to a recent geomagnetic storm.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Philippines accuses China's forces of harassing fisheries vessels in the South China Sea
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Chinese coast guard ships and a Chinese navy helicopter harassed a group of Philippine fisheries vessels conducting a scientific survey in a hotly disputed area of the South China Sea, forcing them to cancel the operation, the Philippine coast guard said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Auschwitz was liberated 80 years ago. The spotlight is on survivors as their numbers dwindle
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The world's focus will be on the remaining survivors of Nazi Germany's atrocities on Monday as world leaders and royalty join them for commemorations on the 80th anniversary of the liberation of Auschwitz.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump visit is 'morale boost': CA fire official
item.description: "I don't know what Trump can give us, but it's a morale boost," said Cal Fire battalion chief Brent Pascua.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Secret Service says it visited Chicago elementary school, not ICE
item.description: Chicago Public Schools officials said in a press conference Friday that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents showed up at an elementary school in the city but were denied entry. But later on Friday, the Secret Service said it visited the school, not ICE.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump arrives in Los Angeles to tour wildfire damage
item.description: Prior to arriving in California Friday, Trump told reporters in North Carolina that the Federal Government would not provide aid to Los Angeles recovery efforts unless new voter ID laws were passed and changes were made to the state's water management system.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KREX 5

item.title: North Dakota’s overturned abortion ban won’t be in effect during appeal, court rules
item.description: North Dakota’s highest court has ruled that the state’s overturned abortion ban will not be enforced during an appeal of a judge’s ruling that struck down the law in September.
item.dc:creator: Jack Dura, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Oregon couple who posed as firefighters during L.A. wildfires charged, face potential jail time
item.description: The Oregon couple who allegedly tried to sneak into a Palisades Fire evacuation zone while impersonating firefighters are facing charges and potential jail time, according to prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WGN 9

item.title: Mediterranean diet improves brain memory: Study
item.description: A Mediterranean diet is beneficial for boosting brain function according to a new study by Tulane University.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Newark mayor says ICE agents raided 'without warrant,' violated Constitution
item.description: New Jersey Democratic officials slammed a workplace raid conducted by Immigration and Customs Enforcement at a seafood market in Newark on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Homeowners in Palisades Fire area angered by bank's decision
item.description: With the devastating Palisades Fire still smoldering in California, Lisa Pelton and some of her neighbors in Mandeville Canyon received an unpleasant notice from their bank: Their home equity lines of credit were being slashed.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Trump ‘very surprised’ by Murkowski, Collins votes against Hegseth
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that he wasn't expecting Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska) and Susan Collins (Maine) to vote against his controversial nominee for secretary of Defense, Army veteran and former Fox News host Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump teases executive order overhauling or nixing FEMA
item.description: President Trump on Friday teased signing an upcoming executive order targeting the Federal Emergency Management Agency that could overhaul or eliminate the entire agency.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington state woman is charged in the fatal shooting of a Border Patrol agent in Vermont
item.description: A 21-year-old Washington state woman faces two weapons charges in connection with the fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: White House says migrant deportation flights with military aircraft have begun
item.description: The Trump administration has started flying immigrants who entered the U.S. illegally out of the country using military aircraft, a White House spokesperson said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: March for Life rally unfolding in Washington, with Vance to make first public appearance since inauguration
item.description: The annual March for Life rally is unfolding in Washington, D.C. on Friday with Vice President JD Vance set to make his first public appearance since being sworn in.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flame thrower and 'flusher' truck: Southern cities get creative to clear snow, ice
item.description: Days after a winter storm dropped ice and record-breaking snow, cleanup efforts were underway Thursday in several major Southern cities such as New Orleans, where crews were removing snow the same way they remove trash, drink cups and plastic beads after Mardi Gras.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Amanda Knox slander conviction upheld in Italy; ex-boyfriend reacts
item.description: Knox's former boyfriend exclusively discusses the conviction in his first interview since the Italian court's decision.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Over 60M kids' data may have been stolen: What you need to know
item.description: More than 62 million students and 9.5 million teachers may have had their data stolen, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KTLA 5

item.title: Activists to 'resist' Trump through know-your-rights campaign
item.description: The pulse of El Paso’s immigrant community can be felt through the calls streaming into a small white “BNHR” building on Piedras Street over the past few days.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: 2025 Oscar nominations: The biggest snubs and surprises
item.description: The members of the film academy voted to honor a few outliers — and overlook a couple of hopeful contenders — at the 97th Academy Awards.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, Fox 5

item.title: Alabama man charged with 11 homicides -- including two mass shootings -- pleads not guilty
item.description: An Alabama man charged with homicide in two separate mass shootings pleaded not guilty in state court on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk mocks Nazi salute accusations with puns
item.description: Elon Musk referenced Adolf Hitler and other Nazi leaders in a social media post filled with puns early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: With Trump pivot back to pro-oil and gas policies, one renewable energy finds favor
item.description: As promised, President Donald Trump began reversing the country's energy policies his first day in office with a spate of orders largely favoring oil, gas and coal. But there is one renewable energy that did find favor: geothermal.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles fires
item.description: The president repeated false claims that the state's fish conservation efforts in the northern part of the state are responsible for fire hydrants running dry in urban areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: U.S. government sues Southwest Airlines, fines Frontier for chronically delayed flights
item.description: “Airlines have a legal obligation to ensure that their flight schedules provide travelers with realistic departure and arrival times," Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Nancy Leftenant-Colon, who was the first Black woman in Army Nurse Corps, has died
item.description: The first Black woman to join the U.S. Army Nurse Corps after the military was desegregated in the 1940s has died. She was 104.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge Chutkan: Trump pardons won't change 'tragic truth' of Jan. 6 Capitol riot
item.description: U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan, who oversaw President Trump's federal election subversion case, wrote in court filings that his sweeping pardons for those charged in connection with the Jan. 6, 2021 Capitol attack could not change the “tragic truth” of that day.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Border Patrol agent killed in Vermont worked at the Pentagon during 9/11, family says
item.description: A U.S. Border Patrol agent who was killed in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border was a military veteran who worked security duty at the Pentagon during the Sept. 11 attacks, his family said.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Border security is popular – but that may be the limit of US immigration consensus: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Many U.S. adults are on board with the idea of beefing up security at the southern border and undertaking some targeted deportations, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX and TIM SULLIVAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s blanket Jan. 6 pardons stun Republicans on Capitol Hill
item.description: President Donald Trump’s sweeping pardons of more than 1,500 people charged with crimes related to the Capitol riot of Jan. 6, 2021, including individuals who assaulted police officers, stunned Republican lawmakers who witnessed firsthand the chaos on Capitol Hill four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Republicans renew efforts to limit people in US illegally from census count
item.description: Republican efforts to exclude people in the U.S. illegally from numbers used to divvy up congressional seats among states have begun anew, with four Republican state attorneys general suing to alter the once-a-decade head count even before President Donald Trump’s second term in office began Monday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A frigid storm drops rare snow on Houston and New Orleans as Florida readies plows in the Panhandle
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A rare frigid storm charged through Texas and the northern Gulf Coast on Tuesday, blanketing New Orleans and Houston with snow that closed highways, grounded nearly all flights and canceled school for more than a million students more accustomed to hurricane dismissals than snow d
item.dc:creator: GERALD HERBERT and DAVID J. PHILLIP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As fire-fanning winds continue, Southern California readies for potential rain and toxic runoff
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Parched Southern California continued to face dangerous winds but could get some badly needed rain this weekend, forecasters said Tuesday, dampening the prospects of another round of killer wildfires though even a small amount of precipitation could could create new challenges lik
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 11-year-old pulls ultra rare Paul Skenes rookie card, Pirates says massive trade deal stands
item.description: The card, which contains the "Debut Patch" worn by Paul Skenes, quickly became a highly-coveted item. After months of search, an 11-year-old has reportedly come up with it.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, WGN 9

item.title: Garth Hudson, The Band's last surviving member, dies at 87
item.description: Garth Hudson, the Band's virtuoso keyboardist and all-around musician who drew from a unique palette of sounds and styles to add a conversational touch to such rock standards as "Up on Cripple Creek," “The Weight” and "Rag Mama Rag," has died at age 87.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
